svg.text: EGYPTIAN ARABIC
HISTORY — EGYPT AND THE MEDITERRANEAN
ARABIC COMES TO EGYPT
639-642 CE: Arab-Muslim conquest of Egypt under Amr ibn al-As
Byzantine (Greek-speaking) Egypt became Arabophone over 2-3 centuries
Coptic spoken as vernacular until ~14th century; then Arabic replaced it
Coptic survived only in church liturgy (still today)
EGYPT AS ARAB CULTURAL CENTER
Cairo (founded 969 CE): center of Islamic scholarship and culture
Al-Azhar University (970 CE): oldest university in continuous operation
Egypt: Arab world's most populous country; cultural output unmatched
Egyptian cinema (1930s+): Hollywood of the Arab world
MODERN EGYPT
~106M population; Cairo one of world's largest cities (~20M metro)
Egyptian Arabic = world's most widely understood Arabic dialect
Arab Spring (2011): Tahrir Square uprising in Egyptian Arabic
GREEK HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
PTOLEMAIC EGYPT: GREEK PHARAOHS
332 BCE: Alexander the Great takes Egypt; founds Alexandria
Ptolemy I (305 BCE): Greek general becomes pharaoh; dynasty lasts 275 years
Cleopatra VII: last Ptolemaic pharaoh; first of her dynasty to learn Egyptian
30 BCE: Rome conquers Egypt; Greek remains co-official for centuries
ALEXANDRIA: THE WORLD CITY
Library of Alexandria: largest knowledge repository of antiquity
Translation: 72 Jewish scholars translated Torah into Greek (Septuagint)
Philo of Alexandria: Jewish philosophy in Greek • Euclid worked here
GREEK TODAY AND EGYPT
~13M Greek speakers; large Greek communities in Alexandria until 1950s
Greek exodus from Egypt after 1952 revolution and Nasser nationalization
Constantine Cavafy: Greek-Egyptian poet; voice of Alexandrian memory
Egypt and Greece: 2500 years of intertwined history on the Mediterranean
PLATE 60d ALT · EGYPTIAN ARABIC / GREEK · HISTORY
